contrastMetadata.transferBoundary: Use prefix-hash state when the answer depends on an earlier cumulative value such as currentPrefix - target; store earliest indices, frequencies, or existence according to the exact result contract.

items.prompt: While scanning arbitrary signed values, currentPrefix is p. Which earlier prefix value proves that a subarray ending here has exact sum k?

options.text: p - k.
p + k.
Only prefix value k.
Only prefix value 0.

feedback.reason: For boundaries j < i, prefix[i] - prefix[j] = k exactly when prefix[j] = prefix[i] - k.
feedback.details: A hash lookup converts that algebraic condition into an expected constant-time state query.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.p_plus_k: That would make the subarray sum -k.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.k_only: The required earlier value depends on the current prefix.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero_only: That recognizes only intervals starting at index 0 when p = k.

items.prompt: The result asks for the number of exact-sum-k subarrays. What should the prefix hash table store?

options.text: The frequency of each previously seen prefix value.
Only whether each prefix occurred.
Only the latest index of each prefix.
The input values sorted ascending.

feedback.reason: Every earlier occurrence of p - k contributes one matching start boundary.
feedback.details: The number of such occurrences must be added to the answer.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.existence_only: One prefix value may occur at several start boundaries, each defining a different subarray.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.latest_only: That loses additional valid starts needed for counting.

items.prompt: The result asks for the longest exact-sum-k subarray. Which prefix metadata is most useful?

options.text: The earliest index at which each prefix value occurred.
Only the number of times each prefix occurred.
Always overwrite with the latest index.
Only one left pointer chosen from sum comparisons.

feedback.reason: For a fixed ending index, the longest interval uses the earliest matching prefix boundary.
feedback.details: The map should preserve the first occurrence rather than overwrite it.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.frequency_only: A count does not identify the farthest start boundary.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.latest_index: A later start makes the resulting interval shorter.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.current_window: Mixed signs provide no safe comparison direction.

items.prompt: Why is prefix value 0 usually seeded before scanning for exact-sum subarrays?

options.text: It represents the boundary before index 0, allowing intervals that begin at the first element.
It prevents prefix sums from becoming negative.
It keeps hash iteration ordered.
It forces all arrays to use one-based indexing.

feedback.reason: If the running prefix itself equals k, then runningPrefix - k equals 0.
feedback.details: The seeded boundary lets that interval be detected without a separate special case.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.avoid_negative: Prefix values may still be negative.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_prefix: Seeding does not sort a table.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_based_only: It is a boundary convention, not an array-index restriction.

items.prompt: For counting non-empty exact-sum-k subarrays, why is the current prefix normally queried before its frequency is incremented?

options.text: It prevents the current boundary from matching itself as a zero-length interval.
Lookup is faster before insertion in every implementation.
Insertion changes the numeric prefix value.
The ordering matters only when values are negative.

feedback.reason: A subarray requires an earlier boundary.
feedback.details: Storing the current prefix after counting ensures only prior prefixes contribute unless empty intervals are explicitly allowed.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.hash_speed: The issue is answer semantics, not a universal performance rule.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.negative_only: Self-matching can matter for k = 0 with any signs.

items.prompt: What causes the same prefix value to occur more than once?

options.text: The elements between two prefix boundaries sum to zero.
Only duplicate individual values.
The input is sorted.
Strictly positive values.

feedback.reason: Equal prefix values mean their difference is zero.
feedback.details: Zeros and mixed positive/negative segments can create repeated prefix state, which is why frequency and earliest-index policies matter.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.duplicate_elements_only: Different sequences can sum to zero without equal elements.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sorted_input: Order alone does not force repeated cumulative sums.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.positive_values: Strictly positive prefixes are strictly increasing and do not repeat.

items.prompt: The running prefix equals k at index i. Which map fact confirms the interval from index 0 through i?

options.text: The seeded zero prefix exists at the boundary before the array.
The current prefix was inserted before lookup.
The key k must have occurred earlier.
An empty window is selected.

feedback.reason: The prefix-difference formula treats the start of the array uniformly with every other boundary.
feedback.details: The sentinel removes a special-case branch.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.current_stored: That would describe the current boundary, not the start boundary.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_key: The needed earlier value is currentPrefix - k = 0.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.window_empty: The interval contains indices 0 through i.

items.prompt: The result asks for the shortest exact-sum-k subarray ending at each position. Which matching prefix occurrence is locally best?

options.text: The latest earlier index with prefix currentPrefix - k.
The earliest such index.
Only the number of occurrences.
The smallest prefix value.

feedback.reason: For a fixed end, length is endBoundary - startBoundary.
feedback.details: The largest valid earlier boundary gives the shortest interval, showing that map payload depends on the result contract.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.earliest_index: That produces the longest interval ending here.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.frequency: A count does not identify the nearest start.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sorted_prefix: Exact equality, not prefix magnitude, determines the start.

items.prompt: Why is a plain prefix array alone insufficient for expected O(n) exact-sum existence over arbitrary signed values?

options.text: It stores cumulative values but still needs an efficient way to find whether p - k occurred earlier.
Prefix differences are invalid for negative values.
A prefix array has no boundary indices.
A hash table creates contiguity.

feedback.reason: The cumulative representation supplies the algebraic key.
feedback.details: The hash table supplies expected constant-time historical lookup; scanning prior prefixes would be quadratic.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.prefix_wrong: The identity is valid for all signs.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.hash_contiguous: Contiguity comes from choosing two prefix boundaries.

items.prompt: Which result contracts require different prefix-hash payloads? Select all that apply.

options.text: Existence may store only whether a prefix occurred.
Counting requires occurrence frequencies.
Longest exact-sum interval requires earliest indices.
Every contract can use one unchanging boolean payload without losing information.

feedback.reason: The hash key can be the same while the associated state changes with the answer contract.
feedback.details: Choosing the payload is part of algorithm design.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_same: Counts and lengths require more metadata.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.existence: A boolean presence test is enough.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.longest: The farthest valid start maximizes length.

items.prompt: Which input features make repeated prefix values possible? Select all that apply.

options.text: Zero-valued elements.
A positive-and-negative segment whose total is zero.
Strictly positive values only.
The seeded pre-array boundary when a later prefix returns to zero.

feedback.reason: Repeated prefix state reflects zero-sum differences.
feedback.details: Strict positivity prevents recurrence.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.mixed_sign_zero_segment: The prefix returns to an earlier value.

items.prompt: Which tasks are natural prefix-hash problems for arbitrary signed arrays? Select all that apply.

options.text: Does any subarray sum to k?
How many subarrays sum to k?
What is the longest subarray summing to k?
What is the maximum sum over every fixed-length adjacent window?

feedback.reason: Exact cumulative-difference objectives fit hash lookup independent of signs.
feedback.details: A sequential fixed-window aggregate does not need historical prefix lookup.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.max_fixed_window: A rolling sum is more direct and uses less state.

items.prompt: Which implementation details are correctness-critical? Select all that apply.

options.text: Initialize the zero prefix with the payload required by the result contract.
For non-empty intervals, use prior prefixes before adding the current boundary.
Preserve earliest, latest, or frequency metadata according to the result.
Depend on the table's iteration order to define interval order.

feedback.reason: Prefix hashing is more than storing sums.
feedback.details: Boundary timing and payload semantics determine which intervals are counted or optimized.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.hash_iteration: Lookup by key, not iteration order, drives correctness.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.seed: It represents intervals beginning at index 0.

items.prompt: Which mistaken claims lose required historical prefix state? Select all that apply.

options.text: Every contiguous exact-sum problem is a sliding-window problem.
Keeping only the current prefix is enough for every exact-sum query.
A frequency alone always identifies the longest interval.
Subarray sum equals the difference of two prefix values.

feedback.reason: The valid identity does not specify all required historical metadata.
feedback.details: Result semantics determine the map state.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.contiguous_window: Mixed signs often require prefix lookup.

items.prompt: Arrange the exact-sum existence workflow.

elements.text: Update the running prefix with the current value.
Test whether runningPrefix - k exists among prior prefixes.
Store the current prefix for later endings.

feedback.reason: The current boundary must compare only with earlier boundaries.
feedback.details: Seeding handles intervals that start at index 0.

items.prompt: Arrange the exact-sum counting workflow.

elements.text: Add frequency[runningPrefix - k] to the answer.

feedback.reason: Every earlier matching prefix contributes one distinct start boundary.
feedback.details: Incrementing afterward preserves the non-empty interval contract.

items.prompt: Arrange the longest exact-sum workflow.

elements.text: Compute the next prefix boundary value.
If prefix - k exists, measure length from its earliest stored boundary.
Store the current prefix only if it has not appeared before.

feedback.reason: The earliest matching boundary maximizes interval length.
feedback.details: Overwriting it would weaken later candidates.

items.prompt: Count all exact-sum-k subarrays using prefix frequencies over n arbitrary signed values. Select expected time and auxiliary space.
constraints: Expected hash lookup and update are O(1).
Up to n + 1 distinct prefix values may be retained.

feedback.reason: Each array value produces one cumulative update and one expected historical lookup.
feedback.details: The table can retain a distinct prefix for every boundary.

items.prompt: Find the longest exact-sum-k subarray using earliest prefix indices over n values. Select expected time and auxiliary space.
constraints: Only the earliest boundary per distinct prefix is retained.

feedback.reason: The payload policy changes, but the number of stored prefix keys remains linear in the worst case.
feedback.details: The scan processes each ending boundary once.

items.prompt: A plain prefix array is built in O(n), then for each of n ending boundaries the algorithm linearly scans all earlier prefixes to find currentPrefix - k. Select total time and auxiliary space.
constraints: No hash or ordered lookup structure is used.

feedback.reason: Cumulative values alone do not guarantee fast historical matching.
feedback.details: The hash state is the mechanism that removes the repeated linear search.

contrastSets.transferBoundary: Use prefix-hash state when the answer depends on an earlier cumulative value such as currentPrefix - target; store earliest indices, frequencies, or existence according to the exact result contract.
